title: MVVM + Hilt: who owns what
objective: Understand why the code is split into Activity / ViewModel / Repository, and what Hilt is doing.

body.p: Without this split, one giant file would fetch data, transform it, and draw the UI all at once — hard to test and hard to change.
Holds a single UiState value (Loading, Success, or Error) in a StateFlow. The Activity just reacts to whatever state it's given.
@HiltViewModel and @Inject mean you never write 'MainViewModel(someRepository)' by hand. Hilt builds every dependency automatically.

lineNotes: @HiltViewModel — tells Hilt this class needs dependency injection.
@Inject constructor(...) — Hilt fills in userRepository for you.
MutableStateFlow — a value that can change over time; the UI observes it and redraws automatically.
viewModelScope.launch — runs the network call on a background coroutine.
result.fold(...) — forces you to handle both success and failure.

quiz.q: What happens if you delete @Inject from MainViewModel's constructor?
options: Hilt no longer knows how to construct MainViewModel, and the app fails to build
The app runs but ignores the repository
